title: Tissue Staining for Imaging Mass Cytometry
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3psj8","text":"This protocol describes the preparation and staining of human FFPE material for multiplex visualization using Imaging Mass Cytometry (IMC). This protocol uses basic standard immunohistochemical staining techniques.  The tissue is incubated with antibodies that have specific affinity for different cells and tissues in the context of a formalin fixed thin section of human tumor material. Antibodies are labeled with heavy metals instead of the typical fluorochromes, and are visualized using the Hyperion Imaging System manufactured by the Fluidigm corporation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cq41i","text":"This technique uses specialized metal tagged antibodies that can be purchased or conjugated by the end user. Dilutions must be empirically determined and validated before using in this protocol. Antibodies are prepared as a cocktail and incubated on the slides all at once.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a20du","text":"All containers for reagents, etc. must be in plastic. Glass hardware must not be used, i.e. graduated cylinders, pipettes, bottles, etc. Glass contains lead at low levels and can interfere with heavy metal tagged antibodies.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"72iom","text":"Slides used must also be kept desiccated and should ideally be freshly cut, do not use any slides that have been cut from blocks for greater than two weeks, if possible.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ceqpt","text":"Oxidation and moisture can interfere with the visualization of the heavy metal tagged antibodies.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"eqkj4","text":"Xylene and ethanol are volatile chemicals with an inhalation risk. All steps performed with Xylene and Ethanol should be in a certified chemical fume hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"16inr","text":"Xylene, histology grade","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ve8p","text":"Ethanol 100% and diluted in diH20","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c56k","text":"Tris EDTA pH 9.0 with 10% Glycerol (can be purchased pre made and add glycerol)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5mmtt","text":"Glycerol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dqjn7","text":"PAP pen","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6qb0a","text":"Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cm3t0","text":"Triton X-100","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cqsd5","text":"Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dua57","text":"DNA intercalator-Ir,   obtain from Fluidigm, catalog # 201192B","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":21,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"u259","text":"Ruthenium Red, Sigma 00541-1G","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8qbjg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"","data":{"concentration":"500","error_concentration":false,"guid":"994158B076EF11EBBC1117EC88C1F2E5","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":29}}}}
steps: Place positively charged slides with 5 µm thick tissue section in a 60 °C oven for 02:00:00
Place slides in fresh xylene for 00:20:00
Rehydrate Place slides in to 100% Ethanol, 00:05:00
Antigen Retrieval Incubate slides for 00:30:00 in antigen retrieval solution from step 4 Make sure the 96.0 °C is maintained consistently throughout the 30 minute incubation.
After incubation, cool slides to 70 °C on the bench top (about 10 minutes)
Wash Place slides in distilled water for 00:10:00 with gentle agitation
Place slides in PBS for 00:10:00 with gentle agitation
Block Nonspecific Staining Encircle each sample with a PAP pen
Blocking solution is 3% BSA in PBS, add just enough to cover the sample and incubate for 00:45:00 at Room temperature in a hydration chamber.
Antibody Staining Pipette 50-100 µL pre-prepared antibody cocktail in 0.5% BSA and incubate Overnight at 4.0 °C in hydration chamber
Wash Wash slides with PBS with 0.2% Triton X-100 for 00:08:00 with gentle agitation
Wash slides in PBS for 00:08:00 with gentle agitation

DNA Intercalator-Ir Prepare Iridium intercalator by diluting 1 to 1000 in PBS ( this dilution may have to determined empirically) Pipette just enough iridium intercalator to cover sample and incubate for 00:30:00 at Room temperature
Remove iridium intercalator by gently tapping edge of slide to a paper towel

Ruthenium Counter Stain Prepare a stock solution of Ruthenium Red by mixing 0.0025 grams in PBS, vortex well to mix Dilute Ruthenium to working concentration of 0.0005% Counterstain tissue by pipetting just enough of the 0.0005% ruthenium red in PBS for 00:10:00 (duration of ruthenium red may need to be determined empirically)
Remove ruthenium red solution by gently tapping edge of slide to paper towel
Wash slides with PBS for 00:08:00 with gentle agitation
Wash Wash slides with distilled water for 00:05:00 with gentle agitation
Air dry slides at room temperature for 00:20:00
Repeat Step 20, 00:08:00
repeat step 22, 00:08:00
repeat Step 13, 00:10:00
repeat Step 15 00:10:00
Slides are now ready for Hyperion Imaging.
Places slides in to 95% Ethanol, 00:05:00
Placed slides in to 80% Ethanol, 00:05:00
Place slides in to 70% Ethanol, 00:05:00
Turn on Water bath for Antigen Retrieval, 96.0 °C
Place slides in to distilled water for 00:05:00 * Note slides may be kept for a longer period at this step, make sure the water bath is the correct temperature before proceeding to the next step.
Prepare Antigen retrieval solution of Tris EDTA pH 9.0 with 10% Glycerol
Place container for slides with above antigen retrieval solution in to water bath to pre-warm
authors: William Gillanders Washington University St Louis
creator: John Herndon
